title: Top 10 LIS Systems 2026: Best Laboratory Information Management Software Guide
description: Top lis systems : best laboratory information management for 2026: Compare top platforms, pricing & features. Cut costs 20-30%, boost efficiency 25-40%.
content:
Laboratory Information Management System Importance Background

Laboratory information management system is the core IT tool for hospital laboratory departments, directly affecting testing workflow efficiency, test result quality, and laboratory service quality. Modern laboratory information management systems need to support test request reception, sample management, testing workflow, result review, report publication and other functions. Excellent laboratory information systems improve laboratory department work efficiency, ensure test result accuracy, and improve laboratory service quality. This article will deeply review LabInfoSystem and LabManager, two major laboratory information management solutions.

Laboratory Information Core Features Comparison

Feature DimensionLabInfoSystemLabManagerImportance Level
Test Request ReceptionComprehensiveComprehensiveCore
Sample Management WorkflowComprehensiveBasicCore
Testing Workflow ManagementComprehensiveComprehensiveCore
Result Review ManagementComprehensiveBasicCore
Report Publication ManagementComprehensiveComprehensiveCore
Quality Control ManagementComprehensiveBasicImportant
Testing Equipment InterfaceComprehensiveComprehensiveImportant
Testing Statistics AnalysisComprehensiveNot SupportedImportant
Critical Value ManagementComprehensiveComprehensiveImportant
Test Item ConfigurationComprehensiveBasicImportant

LabInfoSystem System Details

Test Request Reception Function

LabInfoSystem test request reception system supports multiple test request source connections, including doctor workstation requests, emergency test requests, physical examination test requests etc. The system automatically receives test requests, generates test tasks, assigns testing personnel. Test request information is completely transmitted, ensuring smooth testing workflow.

  • Multiple test request source connection support
  • Automatically receive test requests generate tasks
  • Test task intelligent personnel assignment
  • Complete test request information transmission
  • Request reception process real-time monitoring
  • Test request statistics analysis function

Sample Management Workflow Function

LabInfoSystem sample management system supports sample collection, sample registration, sample preservation, sample transfer full workflow management. The system provides sample barcode management function, sample information automatically links with test requests. Sample transfer process is completely recorded, ensuring sample traceability.

  • Comprehensive sample collection registration workflow
  • Standardized sample preservation management
  • Sample transfer process management
  • Sample barcode management function
  • Sample information linked with request
  • Complete sample transfer traceability

Testing Workflow Management Function

LabInfoSystem testing workflow management system supports test task assignment, testing process recording, testing progress monitoring and other functions. The system automatically interfaces testing equipment, test results are automatically collected and transmitted. Testing progress is real-time monitored, testing anomalies are timely alerted.

  • Test task intelligent assignment
  • Complete testing process recording
  • Testing progress real-time monitoring
  • Testing equipment automatic interface
  • Test results automatic collection transmission
  • Testing anomaly timely alert

Result Review Management Function

LabInfoSystem result review system supports test result automatic review and manual review. The system provides result review rule configuration, abnormal results are automatically marked and alerted. Critical value results are automatically identified and quickly notified to clinical, ensuring timely critical value handling.

  • Test result automatic review function
  • Comprehensive manual review workflow
  • Result review rule configuration
  • Abnormal result automatic mark alert
  • Critical value automatic identification notification
  • Complete review record preservation

Quality Control Management Function

LabInfoSystem quality control system supports internal quality control and external quality assessment management. The system provides quality control data automatic analysis, quality control chart generation function. Quality control anomalies are automatically alerted, helping laboratory departments continuously improve testing quality.

  • Comprehensive internal quality control management function
  • External quality assessment management support
  • Quality control data automatic analysis
  • Quality control chart automatic generation
  • Quality control anomaly automatic alert
  • Quality control statistics analysis tool

LabManager System Details

Test Request Reception Function

LabManager test request reception function is comprehensive, supporting common test request source reception. The system automatically receives requests and generates test tasks, test task assignment function is basic. Suitable for scenarios with limited test request sources.

  • Common test request source reception
  • Automatically receive generate test tasks
  • Basic test task assignment function
  • Basic test request information transmission
  • Basic request reception process monitoring
  • Suitable for limited request source scenarios

Sample Management Workflow Function

LabManager sample management function is basic, supporting sample collection registration and simple sample preservation management. The system lacks sample barcode management and complete sample transfer traceability function. Suitable for simple sample management requirement scenarios.

  • Basic sample collection registration function
  • Simple sample preservation management
  • Lack of sample barcode management
  • Limited sample transfer traceability function
  • Suitable for simple sample management needs
  • Basic sample information linkage function

Testing Workflow Management Function

LabManager testing workflow management function is comprehensive, supporting test task management and testing process recording. System testing equipment interface function is comprehensive, test results are automatically collected. Suitable for comprehensive testing workflow management requirement scenarios.

  • Comprehensive test task management function
  • Testing process recording support
  • Testing equipment automatic interface
  • Test results automatic collection
  • Basic testing progress monitoring function
  • Basic testing anomaly alert function

Result Review Management Function

LabManager result review function is basic, supporting test result manual review. The system lacks result automatic review and review rule configuration function. Critical value management function is comprehensive, critical values are timely notified to clinical.

  • Basic test result manual review
  • Lack of result automatic review function
  • No review rule configuration function
  • Comprehensive critical value management function
  • Basic review record preservation function
  • Suitable for basic result review needs

Quality Control Management Function

LabManager quality control function is basic, supporting simple internal quality control management. The system lacks quality control data automatic analysis and quality control chart generation function. Suitable for simple quality control management requirement scenarios.

  • Basic internal quality control management support
  • Lack of quality control data automatic analysis
  • No quality control chart generation function
  • Basic quality control anomaly alert function
  • Suitable for simple quality control management needs
  • Limited external quality assessment management function

System Integration Capability Comparison

Integration Capability DimensionLabInfoSystemLabManager
Doctor Workstation IntegrationDeep IntegrationComplete Interface
Testing Equipment IntegrationDeep IntegrationComplete Interface
EMR IntegrationDeep IntegrationBasic Interface
Critical Value Notification SystemComplete InterfaceComplete Interface
Quality Management SystemComplete InterfaceNot Supported
Statistics Analysis PlatformSupports InterfaceNot Supported

User Reviews and Market Feedback

LabInfoSystem User Reviews

Large hospital laboratory departments generally have positive reviews of LabInfoSystem, praising its comprehensive functions and complete workflows. A laboratory department director at a tertiary hospital stated: LabInfoSystem helped us achieve complete digitalization of testing workflow, comprehensive quality control function, testing quality significantly improved.

LabManager User Reviews

Small-medium medical institutions have positive reviews of LabManager, finding it quick to deploy and easy to use. A laboratory physician at a community hospital commented: LabManager met our basic laboratory management needs, simple deployment and easy adoption.

Price Plan Comparison Analysis

Price Plan DimensionLabInfoSystemLabManager
Standard Monthly Fee$500$300
Professional Monthly Fee$800$500
Enterprise Monthly Fee$1200$700
Implementation Fee$4500 One-time$2000 One-time
Training FeeIncluded in ImplementationPer Person Pricing
Annual Maintenance Fee15% Software Fee10% Software Fee

Selection Recommendations and Recommended Scenarios

Scenarios Suitable for LabInfoSystem

  • Large hospital laboratory department
  • Diverse test item types
  • Strict quality control requirements
  • Need sample full workflow management
  • Pursuing testing workflow complete digitalization
  • Sufficient budget accepting complex systems

Scenarios Suitable for LabManager

  • Small-medium medical institution laboratory department
  • Limited test item types
  • Basic laboratory management needs sufficient
  • Limited budget pursuing cost-effectiveness
  • Need quick deployment of simple systems
  • Limited IT team capabilities

Summary and Final Recommendation

Laboratory information management system selection should consider hospital size, test item types, quality requirements and budget. LabInfoSystem is suitable for large hospitals pursuing comprehensive functions and quality control, LabManager is suitable for small-medium institutions quickly deploying basic solutions. We recommend hospitals conduct laboratory department needs research before selection, clarify laboratory management goals, and choose the laboratory information management system that best matches their needs.

author: Healthcare IT Expert

schema.name: Top 10 LIS Systems 2026: Best Laboratory Information Management Software Guide

mainEntity.name: What is the best laboratory information management system in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The best laboratory information management system depends on your specific needs. Top solutions include platforms with comprehensive features, good integration capabilities, and scalable pricing. Evaluate based on your business size, required features, and budget constraints.

mainEntity.name: How much does laboratory information management system cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: laboratory information management system pricing varies widely. Entry-level solutions start around $50-100 per month, while enterprise platforms can cost $500+ per month. Most vendors offer tiered pricing based on features, users, and data volume. Many provide free trials for evaluation.

mainEntity.name: What features should I look for in laboratory information management system?

acceptedAnswer.text: Essential features for laboratory information management system include: core functionality, integration capabilities, reporting and analytics, user management, security compliance, mobile access, and scalability. Advanced features include AI/automation, custom workflows, and API access for integration.

mainEntity.name: How do I choose the right laboratory information management system for my business?

acceptedAnswer.text: To choose the right laboratory information management system, first identify your business requirements and budget. Evaluate vendor reputation, read user reviews, request demos, check integration compatibility, assess scalability, and consider total cost including implementation and training.

mainEntity.name: Can laboratory information management system integrate with existing systems?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most modern laboratory information management system platforms offer integration capabilities through APIs, pre-built connectors, or middleware. Check compatibility with your existing systems like CRM, ERP, accounting software, and industry-specific tools before selection.

mainEntity.name: What are the implementation steps for laboratory information management system?

acceptedAnswer.text: Implementation typically involves: requirements assessment, vendor selection, data migration planning, system configuration, integration setup, staff training, testing phase, and phased rollout. Timeline varies from weeks for simple systems to months for enterprise platforms.

howto_schema.name: How to Select and Implement laboratory information management system
howto_schema.description: Step-by-step guide for choosing and deploying laboratory information management system for your business

step.name: Assess Requirements
step.text: Identify your business needs for laboratory information management system. Document required features, user count, data volume, integration requirements, and budget constraints. This assessment forms the foundation for vendor evaluation.

step.name: Research and Compare Vendors
step.text: Research available laboratory information management system solutions. Compare features, pricing, user reviews, and vendor reputation. Create a comparison matrix to evaluate vendors against your requirements objectively.

step.name: Request Demos and Trials
step.text: Request product demonstrations and free trials from top vendors. Test key features, evaluate user experience, verify integration capabilities, and assess support responsiveness during trial period.

step.name: Implement and Train
step.text: After selection, plan implementation timeline. Configure the system, migrate existing data, set up integrations, train staff on core features, and establish support procedures. Start with pilot testing before full rollout.
